name: navigateWithKnowledgeKey
description: Load a static screen from the knowledge base by key. Use this for all welcome journey steps instead of building JSON manually. This is the PREFERRED method for standard screens as it ensures consistency and reduces errors.

key.description: The knowledge key for the screen to load. Each key corresponds to a predefined UI screen with specific templates and options.

key_descriptions.welcome_greeting: Initial welcome screen with 3 glassmorphic bubbles: 'Yes, I'm ready', 'Not just yet', 'Tell me more'
key_descriptions.welcome_tell_me_more: Information screen about trAIn with options to learn more
key_descriptions.qualification_industry: Industry selection screen (MultiSelect) with Technology, Finance, Healthcare, Construction, Something else, I'm not sure
key_descriptions.qualification_industry_text_input: Text input for custom industry entry
key_descriptions.qualification_exploration: Exploration questions for users unsure about their industry
key_descriptions.qualification_role_generic: Generic role selection for other industries
key_descriptions.qualification_role_custom_text_input: Text input for custom role entry
key_descriptions.qualification_priority: Job search priority selection (MultiSelect) - what matters most
key_descriptions.qualification_registration: Registration form with LinkedIn and email options

examples.description: Show welcome greeting at session start
Show industry selection after user is ready
Show role selection for technology industry
Show priority selection after role is chosen
Show registration form as final step

usage_notes: ALWAYS use this function for welcome journey steps instead of manually building navigateToSection payloads
Keys are predefined and tested - do not try to create new keys
Each key returns a complete UI payload with correct templates, bubbles, and progress indicators
The function handles all the complexity of UI state management internally
